# Step 3 — Direct-to-Reading on Share Intent

> **Milestone**: M1.3 | **Task**: T1.3.4.2.1.1
> **Depends on**: E1.3.1 (Universal Share Sheet — COMPLETE)
> **Parallelizable with**: T1.3.4.1.1.1 (markdown stripping), T1.3.4.1.1.2 (tests)
> **Skills to load**: `riverpod-providers`, `flutter-implementing-navigation-and-routing`

Shared context: [E1.3.4 LLM Content Context](../instructions/e1.3.4-llm-content-context.instructions.md)

---

## Task

When content arrives via share intent (from any app, including LLM chats) and `ShareIntentProvider` transitions to `ShareIntentDone`, automatically navigate to the reading screen with the extracted document. Currently, the state machine reaches `done` but nothing acts on it — the user has to manually find the content.

**Design decisions**:
- Shared content goes straight to reading (no library stop)
- Back navigation from share-initiated reading returns to library (`/`)
- Use `ClipboardDocument` wrapper for ephemeral (session-only) content
- `ShareIntentProvider` state cleared after navigation to prevent re-triggering

---

## Files to Modify

### 1. `lib/app.dart` — Add share intent navigation listener

The `_ContentLifecycleObserver` already exists as a `ConsumerStatefulWidget` that watches app lifecycle. Add a `ref.listen` on `shareIntentProvider` to navigate when content is ready.

**In `_ContentLifecycleObserverState.initState`**, add a post-frame callback to set up the listener:

```dart
@override
void initState() {
  super.initState();
  _lifecycleListener = AppLifecycleListener(onResume: _onResume);

  // Also check once on initial launch.
  WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback((_) {
    _onResume();
    _listenForShareIntent();
  });
}
```

**Add the listener method:**

```dart
/// Navigates to reading screen when share intent produces a document.
void _listenForShareIntent() {
  ref.listenManual(shareIntentProvider, (previous, next) {
    if (next is ShareIntentDone) {
      _navigateToReading(next);
    }
  });
}

void _navigateToReading(ShareIntentDone state) {
  final doc = ClipboardDocument(
    title: state.content.title ?? 'Shared Content',
    document: state.document,
  );

  // Clear state to prevent re-triggering on rebuild.
  ref.read(shareIntentProvider.notifier).clear();

  // Navigate to reading screen. Use go() not push() so back returns to library.
  if (mounted) {
    GoRouter.of(context).go('/read-clipboard', extra: doc);
  }
}
```

**Add required imports** at the top of the file:

```dart
import 'package:go_router/go_router.dart';
import 'package:runthru/core/clipboard_document.dart';
```

Note: `share_intent_provider.dart` is already imported. The `ShareIntentDone` class is already exported from that file.

### 2. `lib/features/content/providers/share_intent_provider.dart` — Content type routing

In `_extractContent`, when the content type is `text` or `htmlText`, the normaliser should detect markdown. LLM apps share as `text/plain` but the content IS markdown.

**Modify the `SharedContentType.text` case** to auto-detect markdown:

```dart
case SharedContentType.text:
  // LLM apps (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) share as text/plain but
  // content is typically markdown-formatted. Detect and route.
  final contentType = _looksLikeMarkdown(content.data)
      ? ContentType.markdown
      : ContentType.plainText;
  return ContentNormaliser.normalise(content.data, contentType);
```

**Add the detection helper** as a private static method on the class:

```dart
/// Heuristic: does this text look like markdown?
///
/// Checks for common markdown indicators that plain prose wouldn't have.
/// Conservative — only triggers on clear markdown signals.
static bool _looksLikeMarkdown(String text) {
  // Check first 2000 chars to avoid scanning huge documents.
  final sample = text.length > 2000 ? text.substring(0, 2000) : text;

  var signals = 0;
  if (RegExp(r'^#{1,6}\s', multiLine: true).hasMatch(sample)) signals++;
  if (sample.contains('```')) signals++;
  if (RegExp(r'\*\*[^*]+\*\*').hasMatch(sample)) signals++;
  if (RegExp(r'^\s*[-*+]\s', multiLine: true).hasMatch(sample)) signals++;
  if (RegExp(r'\[.+\]\(.+\)').hasMatch(sample)) signals++;

  // Two or more signals → treat as markdown.
  return signals >= 2;
}
```

---

## What NOT to Modify

- Do NOT add a new route. The existing `/read-clipboard` route handles this perfectly — it takes a `ClipboardDocument` via `state.extra`.
- Do NOT touch `app_router.dart`. The route already exists.
- Do NOT touch Android/iOS native code. The share plumbing is complete.
- Do NOT touch `ParallaxReadingScreen`. It already accepts `ClipboardDocument`.

---

## Verification

```powershell
dart analyze lib/app.dart lib/features/content/providers/share_intent_provider.dart
flutter test test/features/content/
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] When `ShareIntentProvider` transitions to `ShareIntentDone`, app navigates to reading screen
- [ ] Navigation uses `go_router` (`GoRouter.of(context).go()`) — no `Navigator.push()`
- [ ] Reading screen receives the extracted document via `ClipboardDocument` wrapper
- [ ] If app is cold-started via share intent, reading screen appears after extraction
- [ ] If app is already open, reading screen replaces current route (not pushed on top)
- [ ] Back navigation from share-initiated reading returns to library (`/`)
- [ ] `ShareIntentProvider` state is cleared after navigation (no re-trigger on rebuild)
- [ ] Plain text shared from LLM apps is auto-detected as markdown when 2+ signals present
- [ ] Plain text without markdown signals is processed as `ContentType.plainText` (no regression)
- [ ] `dart analyze` reports zero issues on all modified files
- [ ] No files outside `lib/app.dart` and `lib/features/content/providers/share_intent_provider.dart` are modified

## Refusal Clause

You may refuse if:
- `_ContentLifecycleObserver` has been refactored since what's documented in the shared context
- The `/read-clipboard` route no longer exists or its API has changed
- `ClipboardDocument` constructor has changed from `ClipboardDocument({title, document})`
